description_long: This is a rustic wooden barn with a steeply pitched, thatched gable roof that extends well beyond the walls, providing extra shelter. The structure is constructed from rough-hewn timber planks arranged horizontally, with visible support beams protruding at the roofline. The front features a wide, open doorway without a visible door, allowing easy access to the interior, which appears to have a dirt or straw floor. The roof is capped with a decorative wooden crosspiece at the ridge, a traditional feature in some rural barn designs. There are no visible windows, and the sides and rear are solid, emphasizing the barn's utilitarian function. The overall appearance suggests a historical or vernacular agricultural building, likely used for storage or sheltering livestock.
description: A rustic wooden barn with a steep thatched gable roof, open front doorway, and no visible windows. The structure is made of horizontal timber planks and features a decorative roof ridge.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide open doorway, horizontal timber siding, and decorative crosspiece at the roof ridge.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the long, overhanging thatched roof and solid wooden wall with no openings.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the solid wooden wall, horizontal planking, and the continuation of the gable roof.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, similar to view 1, showing the extended thatched roof and unbroken wooden siding.
attribute_roof_type: steeply pitched gable roof with thatched covering and decorative ridge crosspiece
attribute_siding_pattern: horizontal timber planks with visible support beams
attribute_door_style: single wide open doorway, no visible door or hardware
attribute_window_arrangement: no visible windows on any side
